About: It is about ethnic nationalistic political ideology.
Affects: It affects the defense of the Afrikaans language.
It affects the theoretical formulation of Afrikaner nationalism.
It affects the economic conditions of the Afrikaners.
It affects the uptake of a new Christian republican identity.
It affects South African opposition to the country's involvement in wars.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Victorian era.
It is associated with the Great Trek.
It is associated with the First and Second Boer Wars.
It is associated with anti-British sentiment.
It is associated with anti-communism.
It is associated with opposition to South Africa's entry into World War I.
It is associated with the Broederbond secret society.
It is associated with the National Party.
It is associated with the Federation of Afrikaans Cultural Organisations.
It is associated with the Institute for Christian National Education.
BelongsTo: It belongs to ethnic nationalistic political ideology.
CausedBy: It is caused by significant events in Afrikaner history.
It is caused by the resulting anti-British sentiment.
It is caused by anti-communism that developed among Afrikaners.
It is caused by opposition to South Africa's entry into World War I.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the Afrikaner people's history.
It consists of the defense of the Afrikaans language.
CreatedAround: It was created around the Victorian era.
CreatedIn: It was created in Southern Africa.
CreatedTo: It was created to respond to significant events in Afrikaner history.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by the Broederbond secret society.
It was developed by the National Party.
It was developed by Nicolaas Johannes Diederichs.
It was developed by Geoffrey Cronjé.
It was developed by Afrikaner nationalist intelligentsia.
It was developed by J. B. M. Hertzog.
It was developed by Broederbond members.
DevelopedIn: It was developed in Southern Africa.
It was developed in the 1930s and 1940s.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Stephanus Jacobus du Toit.
HasA: It has a destiny to rule South Africa.
It has a God-ordained right to exist as separate entities.
It has a radical nationalistic policy.
It has a common root in religio-nationalism.
It has a common root in Pan-Germanism.
It has a slogan "South Africa first."
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by ethnic nationalistic political ideology.
It is characterized by opposition to South Africa's entry into World War I.
It is characterized by civil religion.
It is characterized by the defense of the Afrikaans language.
It is characterized by republicanism.
It is characterized by Afrikaner Calvinism.
It is characterized by anti-British sentiment.
It is characterized by anti-communism.
It is characterized by the notion that Afrikaners were a distinct nationality.
It is characterized by the destiny of the volk to rule South Africa.
Influenced: It influenced the theoretical formulation of Afrikaner nationalism.
It influenced the formulation of a radical nationalistic policy.
It influenced the National Party's politics.
It influenced the usage of Afrikaans instead of Dutch.
It influenced the translation of the Bible into Afrikaans.
It influenced the National Party's rise to power.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by significant events in Afrikaner history.
It was influenced by the Great Trek.
It was influenced by the First and Second Boer Wars.
It was influenced by the resulting anti-British sentiment.
It was influenced by anti-communism.
It was influenced by opposition to South Africa's entry into World War I.
It was influenced by Afrikaner Calvinism.
It was influenced by Abraham Kuyper.
It was influenced by the Enlightenment.
It was influenced by Kuyperian theology.
IsA: It is an ethnic nationalistic political ideology.
It is a radical nationalistic policy.
KnownFor: It is known for its ethnic nationalistic political ideology.
It is known for its development during the Victorian era.
It is known for its influence on Afrikaner history.
It is known for its association with the Broederbond and National Party.
It is known for its role in formulating apartheid.
It is known for its religious underpinnings.
It is known for its secular formulations.
It is known for its opposition to British hegemony.
It is known for its promotion of Afrikaner identity.
It is known for its use of popular media.
LocatedIn: It is located in Southern Africa.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Dutch Reformed Church.
It is a member of the National Party.
It is a member of the Broederbond.
It is a member of the Federation of Afrikaans Cultural Organisations.
It is a member of the Institute for Christian National Education.
It is a member of the White Workers' Protection Association.
It is a member of the Afrikaner Bond.
It is a member of the Gereformeerde Kerke van Zuid-Afrika.
It is a member of the Ossewabrandwag.
It is a member of the Stormjaers.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the inter-war period.
It occurred during World War II.
OccurredAround: It occurred around the Victorian era.
PartOf: It is part of ethnic nationalistic political ideology.
PublishedAs: It was published as "Nationalism as a Worldview and Its Relationship to Internationalism".
PublishedBy: It was published by Die Afrikaanse Patriot newspaper.
It was published by Burger Boekhandel publishing house.
UsedAs: It is used as a civil religion.
It is used as a basis for political, economic and cultural spheres.
It is used as a solution for total segregation.
It is used as a justification for separation and rule.
It is used as a means to protect the nation.
It is used as a tool to diffuse nationalism.
UsedFor: It is used for the defense of the Afrikaans language.
It is used for the preservation and protection of historical nations.
It is used for the formulation of Afrikaner nationalistic ideology.
It is used for the drastic reordering of the South African demographic map.
It is used for the creation of separate healthy cities for the non-whites.
It is used for the improvement of the economic conditions of the Afrikaners.
It is used for the diffusion of nationalism.
It is used for the promotion of Afrikaner identity.
It is used for the translation of the Bible into Afrikaans.

It is used in the Federation of Afrikaans Cultural Organisations.
It is used in the Institute for Christian National Education.
It is used in the White Workers' Protection Association.
It is used in the Afrikaner Bond.
It is used in the Gereformeerde Kerke van Zuid-Afrika.
UsedTo: It is used to preserve and protect spheres from liberalism and revolutionary ideologies.
It is used to define political, economic and cultural spheres.
It is used to reinterpret Afrikaner history.
It is used to refuse a "British-designed" South Africa.
It is used to argue for total segregation.
It is used to reject British hegemony.
It is used to propose the drastic reordering of the South African demographic map.
It is used to improve the economic conditions of the Afrikaners.
It is used to diffuse nationalism.
It is used to promote the usage of Afrikaans instead of Dutch.
WrittenBy: It was written by Stephanus Jacobus du Toit.
